title: BTC Managed Exposure Persistent Floor Lead
hypothesis: A graduated BTC core regime with an 80/70/10 exposure ladder can stay partially deployed during risk-off while still beating HODL on risk-adjusted metrics and materially reducing drawdown.

notes: This fixed comparison reuses the long-history Coinbase BTC/USD dataset that already anchors the crypto research lane.
The benchmark remains perfect buy-and-hold BTC over the exact same tape.
This is a fixed finalist comparison contract, not an open-ended search spec.
The tactical layer is evaluated only as an additive top-up to the graduated core, never as a standalone replacement for managed BTC exposure.

exposure_model.description: Graduated BTC core with a 10% risk-off floor plus a tactical top-up overlay that can lift total exposure to 100% but never override the floor.

notes: This run should emit a fixed comparison bundle rather than a multi-variant sweep leaderboard.
Uses the same production-minded crypto fee/slippage assumptions as the earlier BTC bench work.
Success is defined as disciplined BTC participation, not beating HODL on raw return.
This persistent-floor finalist is expected to preserve a small BTC foothold even in risk-off while still improving Sharpe and Calmar versus passive HODL.
This candidate extends the risk-adjusted managed-BTC family with a small nonzero risk-off floor.
It exists to test whether the crypto sleeve can stay deployable without giving up the disciplined drawdown story.
